colette_default.speaker: Pink Panther
colette_default.text: Bonjour, madame. I'm looking for information about the Louvre.

colette_default.speaker: Madame Colette
colette_default.text: Ah, ze Louvre! Ze largest art museum in ze world!

colette_default.speaker: Madame Colette
colette_default.text: It was originally a royal palace, built in ze 12th century.

colette_default.speaker: Madame Colette
colette_default.text: Today it holds over 380,000 objects and 35,000 works of art!

colette_default.speaker: Pink Panther
colette_default.text: Impressive. And what about that glass pyramid?

colette_default.speaker: Madame Colette
colette_default.text: Ah, ze Pyramid! Designed by I.M. Pei in 1989. Very controversial at first!

colette_default.speaker: Madame Colette
colette_default.text: People said it did not belong. Now it is ze most famous entrance in ze world.

colette_default.speaker: Madame Colette
colette_default.text: And of course, ze Eiffel Tower behind you — built in 1889 for ze World Fair.

colette_default.speaker: Madame Colette
colette_default.text: Gustave Eiffel designed it. It was meant to be temporary — just 20 years!

colette_default.speaker: Madame Colette
colette_default.text: But zey kept it because it was perfect for radio transmissions.

colette_default.speaker: Pink Panther
colette_default.text: A temporary tower that became permanent. How fitting.

colette_default.speaker: Madame Colette
colette_default.text: Ze museum is just down ze street, to ze right. Enjoy, monsieur!

colette_post.speaker: Madame Colette
colette_post.text: Ze Louvre is to ze right, monsieur. You cannot miss ze pyramid!

curator_default.speaker: Curator Beaumont
curator_default.text: Ah, a visitor! Welcome to ze Musee du Louvre.

curator_default.speaker: Pink Panther
curator_default.text: Thank you. I'm investigating something... unusual.

curator_default.speaker: Curator Beaumont
curator_default.text: Unusual? In ze Louvre, everything is extraordinary!

curator_default.speaker: Curator Beaumont
curator_default.text: Zis hall contains some of ze finest works in history.

curator_default.speaker: Curator Beaumont
curator_default.text: Ze Mona Lisa, of course — painted by Leonardo da Vinci around 1503.

curator_default.speaker: Curator Beaumont
curator_default.text: Her smile has puzzled visitors for over 500 years!

curator_default.speaker: Curator Beaumont
curator_default.text: And ze Venus de Milo — a Greek sculpture from around 100 BC.

curator_default.speaker: Pink Panther
curator_default.text: Actually, I'm looking for a specific painting. A boy back at camp keeps drawing it.

curator_default.speaker: Curator Beaumont
curator_default.text: A boy... drawing paintings he has never seen? How peculiar.

curator_default.speaker: Curator Beaumont
curator_default.text: Describe what he draws, and perhaps I can identify it.

curator_default.speaker: Pink Panther
curator_default.text: A woman's face. Ornate golden frames. He says something is 'missing' from it.

curator_default.speaker: Curator Beaumont
curator_default.text: Mon Dieu... zat sounds like ze portrait in Room 7.

curator_default.speaker: Curator Beaumont
curator_default.text: A painting zat was recently restored. Ze restorer found a hidden symbol underneath.

curator_default.speaker: Curator Beaumont
curator_default.text: Perhaps your boy senses what was hidden. Take zis postcard of ze painting.

curator_default.speaker: Curator Beaumont
curator_default.text: If he sees ze complete image, perhaps his mind will settle.

curator_post.speaker: Curator Beaumont
curator_post.text: Ze postcard should help your young friend.

curator_post.speaker: Curator Beaumont
curator_post.text: Ze mysteries of art connect us in ways we do not understand.
